Notice Of Motion And Mo'Tion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Written Stipulation Of All Parties
Off Calendar Nov-17-2021 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ZORANA BREED's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT.

Defendant's motion to set aside the default judgment is granted. The court notes that the motion does not appear to address the setting aside of the default. (The papers generally refer to the "default" without differentiating but the captions on the papers say "motion to vacate default judgment," although the notice states that Defendant is moving "for an order to set aside the default issued in the case.") See e.g., Weil & Brown, et al., California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 5:403, 5:413 (Rutter: 2020).

CCP section 473(d) provides that the court "may, on motion of either party . . . set aside any void judgment or order." "[C]ompliance with the statutory procedures for service of process is essential to establish personal jurisdiction. Thus, a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void. Under section 473 (d), the court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service." Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544 (internal citations omitted).

Defendant was not served. While Plaintiff is correct that there is a presumption of valid service, that presumption is rebutted by Defendant's declaration that she does not fit the description of the individual described in the proof of service. Zorana Breed Decl. ISO Mot., 6. "Here, the proof of service filed by plaintiff included the declaration of a registered process server averring that he personally served defendant . . . Defendant declared that he was not served. Though the trial court was not required to accept this self-serving evidence contradicting the process server's declaration, the proof of service on its face indicates that the process server did not comply with the rules governing service. It . . . describes defendant as an Asian with black hair, a description that does not fit defendant. The proof of service was therefore untruthful." Am. Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 390.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)
